sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 78

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 78:1
items.html: (to the question) A seal [Chotam] carries the meaning of a boundary, and the meaning of engraving — like the seal that is impressed upon wax, whose markings remain sunk and engraved in the wax. And there are two seals in the Nukva, which are the Yesod and the Malchut in her: the Yesod is the inner seal, and from it the second, outer seal is stamped, in the secret of "the inner seal within the outer seal." The inner one is called Zion, which is the Yesod in her; and the outer one is called Jerusalem, which is the Malchut in her. And they have Katnut and Gadlut. For at first, while she cleaves to ZA [Zeir Anpin] Achor be Achor [back-to-back], she takes these two seals, Yesod and Malchut, from ZA, in the secret of the engraving of the Gevurot of the discernment of "bent fire"; and then both are judgments, and therefore both are called Jerusalem — for even the Yesod in her is then called Jerusalem, since even the inner seal is still considered like the outer seal, being discernments of judgments, and the difference between them is not yet so recognizable. But afterwards, after the Nesira [sawing apart], when her fallen Achoraim [posterior] are returned to her for the building of her Partzuf, and she has the ten Sefirot of Gadlut in completeness, then the NHY [Netzah, Hod, Yesod] of Ima clothe in her, which are three names of EKYEH: with Yods, with Alephs, with Heys — and these three fillings are, in Gematria, Chotam [seal], with the inclusive one. This intimates that she takes the two above seals again, by means of Ima, and with this the seals are completed in the secret of a seal within a seal, for now they have attained the discernment of the Gadlut in them. And the five Gevurot of Ima, which are the secret of the second MANTZEPACH that the Nukva now receives, are guarded within the Yesod of the Nukva, in the discernment of a seal within a seal, in the secret of "the wine that gladdens." And these two discernments of seals that she received from Ima are the Yesod and Malchut of Gadlut, and both are called Zion, relative to the Yesod and Malchut of Katnut that she received from ZA, which are called Jerusalem, as above. But the Gadlut and Katnut join together; hence the Yesod in her is now called Zion, and the inner seal, and the present Malchut is called Jerusalem, and the outer seal. In this way the guarding of "a seal within a seal" is explained: for only in Gadlut are the essence of the Gevurot received, called "the wine that gladdens," which need superior guarding, a seal within a seal. And with this all the variations of language found in the words of the Rav are explained. (p. 781, Item 73; and Item 75; and Item 72; and p. 794, Item 92 and Item 93; and Item 95)
